text: The patient is positioned in Trendelenburg with the right side down. The surgeon performs a medial to lateral dissection of the left mesocolon, exposing, ligating, and dividing the IMA pedicle. A total mesorectal excision is carried out down to the pelvic floor, stopping proximal to the levators. The proximal sigmoid is divided with an endoscopic stapler, and an end colostomy is matured. The patient is repositioned in Kraski position for a wide local excision of the perineum, maintaining dissection in the extra levator plane. The posterior vaginal wall is excised en bloc, and intraoperative frozen sections from vaginal wall margins are sent for histopathology, all returning negative. The dissection includes macroscopically abnormal skin and deepens cephalad to connect with the abdominal dissection.
The surgeon reconstructs the posterior vaginal wall using interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures, followed by primary closure of the perineal defect.
